headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words lettered A to E, choose the word or group of words that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word and that will at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	There is no doubt that **pride** is less desirable a quality than ...................
2.	Few people prefer the **rural** to the ............. areas
3.	Emeka is quite **refined** in his manners but his colleague is rather ..................
4.	Instead of helping to **preserve** our culture, the youth engage in activities that ................. it
5.	We should regard donations as ................. and not **compulsory**
6.	The **affluence** of the politicians contrasts with the .................. of the masses
7.	The importation of rice is **permitted** while that of wheat is ................
8.	The goal lifted the team from the **agony** of defeat to the ............... of victory
9.	One species of the animal has gone into **extinction** while the other is still in ...................
10.	The Council **ratified** the controversial recommendation which we thought would be ....................

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to E, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences

S2.qs: 11.	He was sent to the Remand Home in the hope that the place would ..................... him
12.	Since there was no further comments the chairman called for the ................... of the minutes of the last meeting
13.	There was a ten-minute .................. during the stage-performance of the play
14.	The judge restrained the landlord from .................. the tenant
15.	The soldiers pledged their ................. loyalty to their commander
16.	Many children are suffering from .................. because of poor feeding
18.	The audience gave the actor a standing ................... for his beautiful performance
19.	If the customer fails to honour his pledge again, his ............... will surely be in doubt
20.	The judge warned that he would not tolerate any interruption of the .................. of the court

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretations of the sentence is given. Choose tho interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence

S3.qs: 21.	I can make a little out of Tolu’s story. This means that

opts.q0: A. I don’t understand Tolu’s story
opts.q1: B. I quite appreciate Tolu’s story

S3.qs: 22.	Bola promised to pay Sade in her own coin. This means that Bola

opts.q1: B. defaulted in the repayment of a loan from Sade
opts.q2: C. took a loan from Sade
opts.q3: D. threatened to take revenge on Sade

S3.qs: 23.	Contrary to our expectations, the principal rose to the occasion. This means that the principal

opts.q0: A. was the chairman of the occasion
opts.q1: B. made the most beautiful speech at the occasion
opts.q2: C. proved himself able to handle the situation
opts.q3: D. was invited as the guest-of-honour despite our objection

S3.qs: 24.	The workers accused the manager of lying to take the bread out of their mouth. This means that the manager

opts.q0: A. wanted to deprive them of their means of livelihood
opts.q2: C. did not tolerate lazy workers
opts.q3: D. did not show enough sympathy with problems

S3.qs: 25.	The journalist said his story on the minister was made from observation at close quarters. This means that the

opts.q0: A. journalist wrote the story within short notice
opts.q1: B. story was based on an interview conducted in the minister’s quarters
opts.q2: C. journalist had access to certain confidential materials
opts.q3: D. journalist observed the minister intimately

S3.qs: 26.	Amaka was the cynosure of all eyes at the party. This means that

opts.q0: A. everybody looked down at her
opts.q1: B. everybody was surprised that she attended the party
opts.q2: C. she was notorious for her behaviour

S3.qs: 27.	Olu often goes about with a chip on his shoulder. This means that Olu

opts.q0: A. behaves in a very rude manner
opts.q1: B. looks exactly like his father
opts.q2: C. moves about with the injury on his shoulder
opts.q3: D. walks with an air of pride and defiance

S3.qs: 28.	Adamu just can’t help giving alms to the poor. This means that Adamu

opts.q0: A. gives alms to the poor compulsively
opts.q1: B. thinks poor people do not deserve pity
opts.q3: D. does and give alms to the poor

S3.qs: 29.	On a crucial issue like this, I am sure that Peter, as usual, will sit on the fence. This means that Peter will

opts.q0: A. speak out his true feelings
opts.q1: B. not commit himself to any side
opts.q2: C. be relaxing in his house instead of speaking out
opts.q3: D. sit on the wall between the two parties

S3.qs: 30.	When Nosa applied for a loan, the bank manager requested him to put his cards on the table. This means that

opts.q0: A. the bank manager requested for a bribe
opts.q1: B. Nosa should show that he has the ability to repay the money
opts.q2: C. Nosa should state openly and honestly what he needed the loan for
opts.q3: D. the bank manager is unwilling to grant Nosa any loan

S3.qs: 31.	Considering that he earns a very fat salary, the engineer ought to have built a house by now. This means that the engineer

opts.q0: A. has not built a house because he is still considering his salary
opts.q1: B. has not built a house though he earns a handsome salary
opts.q2: C. will spend all the money he earns if he wants to build a house
opts.q3: D. wants to build a house but he has not earned sufficient money

S3.qs: 32.	John is too courageous a man to be dispirited by that setback. This means that John

opts.q0: A. experienced some difficulties but is still determined to succeed
opts.q1: B. gave up after the setback
opts.q2: C. lost the will to continue after the difficulties he encountered
opts.q3: D. gave up the struggle because he did not have enough courage

S3.qs: 33.	Hassan should have had that promotion. This means that Hassan

opts.q2: C. will not have had the promotion
opts.q3: D. did not have the promotion

S3.qs: 34.	By December this year, I shall have moved from Ibadan to Port Harcourt. This means that I will

opts.q0: A. move from Ibadan after this December
opts.q1: B. be in Ibadan until the end of December
opts.q2: C. be in Port Harcourt this December
opts.q3: D. move to Port Harcourt after this December

S4.headd: From the words or group of words lettered AtoE below each of the following sentences, choose the one that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words as it is used in the sentence

S4.qs: 35.	The minister explained that the execution of the project had been **impeded** by lack of funds
36.	The clerk was dismissed for **dereliction** of duty
37.	We had to **prevail** upon him to accept the offer
38.	He worked very hard all through his life but he died in **penury**
40.	He was so **engrossed** in his reading that he didn’t notice when I entered
41.	All that were left after the riots were the **charred** remains of vehicles and houses
42.	The manager warned the workers that the company would no longer tolerate their **incessant** demand for salary increase
43.	The social critic was asked to **recant** the allegations he made against the government
44.	The politician has an **inordinate** ambition to rule the country
45.	The robber’s two jail terms are to run **concurrently**
46.	A journalist would do well not to express **prejudicial** view
47.	A reasonable man will never refuse to **review** his action if it becomes necessary

S5.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that best completes each of the following sentences

S5.qs: 48.	His account of the incident isn’t true, he just ................
49.	The newly married couple are not ............... with each other
50.	Ibrahim is gradually ..................... his problems
51.	Jide was absent from the meeting yesterday, .....................
52.	He got into trouble in an attempt to conceal the truth ............... me
53.	He was accompanied ........................ his friend to the party
54.	My father said he once saw snake ................... a chicken
55.	Mary ................. passed the examination without the extra lessons she had
56.	Many people are now used to ................. without lunch
57.	....................... round the house, I observed that there were more broken louvers than had been reported
58.	The beleaguered soldiers skillfully .................. themselves out of trouble
59.	................... so dejected, he decided to stay at home
60.	How I wish she ..................... the note in time
61.	The principal, with all the teachers .................... and students
62.	What ....................... these last three years?

opts.q0: A. are you doing with yourself
opts.q1: B. have you been doing with yourself
opts.q2: C. you have been doing with yourself
opts.q3: D. had you being doing with yourself

S5.qs: 63.	The government has been urged to provide more facilities for training ................

opts.q0: A. physically disabled and mentally retardeds
opts.q1: B. the physically disabled and mentally retardeds
opts.q2: C. the physically disabled and mentally retarded
opts.q3: D. physically disabled and mentally retarded

S5.qs: 64.	I am looking forward ..................... from you before long
65.	The road cannot be constructed without ...........................

opts.q1: B. huge machines those earth moving

S5.qs: 66.	It was reported that he stole the radio ....................
67.	He willed his property to the poor ....................
68.	I would think Sam is .................... to join the senior football team
69.	Tolu: I like playing table-tennis Yemi: ..........................
70.	The bicycle ..................... on the wet grass for several days
71.	Didn’t I give Ada the pen a while ago? ........................
72.	You didn't knock before you entered .................
73.	Our tour of London came to an end all .................... soon
74.	Ranti: 'Must we finish cracking all the nuts before noon?' Ike: No, we ........................
75.	Each of the players .................. to receive a gift of N 10,000.00

S6.headd: In the following passage the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, five choices are offered in columns lettered A to E. For each question choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passage.
PA: If the advantages of international trade and division of labour are as strong and --76-- as economists assert, the student of international trade may well be puzzled by the --77-- to which governments impose --78-- on their --79-- trade.
The actions of governments, however, are not swayed entirely by economic --80--. When a country aims at economic self-sufficiency, the reasons are generally --81--.
For example, a country may wish to --82-- its farming industry in order to be able to feed itself in time of war, or it may try to build an iron and --83-- industry as part of an --84-- programme.
If a conference is called to consider a reduction of --85--, a country that had imposed no restrictions on imports would find itself at a --86--, since it would have no --87-- to offer.

S6.headd: In the following passage the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, five choices are offered in columns lettered A to E. For each question choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passage.
PB: I made a visit to the University Teaching Hospital the other day. At the -88- Department where accident --89-- are treated, the doctors were battling to save the life of a man who had just been knocked down in a road accident.

They --90-- the injured man thoroughly with the aid of several --91-- instruments such as the --92-- used in measuring body temperature and the --93-- used in listening to the sound of the heart and the lungs.
By using --94--, several internal parts of the patient’s body were photographed --95-- and pain-killing injections were given. His bleeding was stopped.
The nurses --96-- his wounds and he was carried on a --97-- to the special care --98-- where those under --99-- care are kept. The doctor asked the nurses to keep the patient under close --100--.
